title: rarely velvety chinese fish bao
description: a light chinese dish featuring thai fish sauce served in a long-fermentation dough with a citrusy and velvety texture

ingredients.name: long-fermentation dough (homemade or store-bought)
ingredients.notes: each piece should be about 100 grams

instructions: preheat a dry pan over medium heat and toast the long-fermentation dough pieces until lightly browned on both sides, about 2 minutes per side
transfer the toasted dough to a plate and let cool slightly
in the same pan, add 1 tsp of sesame oil and cook the tilapia fillets for about 2 minutes per side, until opaque and cooked through
transfer the cooked tilapia to a clean plate and set aside
in a small bowl, mix together chopped scallions, chopped cilantro, grated ginger, lime juice, and thai fish sauce
brush the sesame oil on the toasted dough pieces and place them on a serving plate or tray
top each dough piece with a cooked tilapia fillet and spoon some of the citrusy herb mixture over the fish
